ctxMarket: Attention is focused on Solana’s growing on-chain activity, even as SOL’s price remains in a tight range. Political involvement by Solana-backed groups is drawing headlines, alongside broader crypto policy developments.
ctxTalking:
• Solana Institute and Multicoin Capital are funding a super PAC targeting the Ohio Senate race.
• Solana’s network usage is rising, but price momentum has not followed.
• The potential next Fed Chair reportedly holds Solana in his crypto portfolio.
ctxMatters:
Political advocacy by Solana-affiliated groups signals deeper engagement with U.S. regulation and policymaking.  
Sustained network activity without price movement may shape perceptions of Solana’s utility and future growth.  
High-profile holders, like a Fed Chair candidate, could influence mainstream visibility and regulatory tone.
ctxSignal:
Signal: Solana-backed funding in U.S. elections and increased network usage highlight real-world and on-chain traction.
Noise: Speculation about immediate price “catch up” lacks clear supporting evidence.
ctxGlossary:
Super PAC — a political action committee that can raise and spend unlimited funds to influence elections  
On-chain activity — transactions and usage recorded directly on a blockchain

readMore.title: Solana Institute-backed super PAC pours $8 million against Sherrod Brown in Ohio race
Solana Policy Institute-backed PAC spends millions to jam Sherrod Brown's Senate run
Solana’s usage booms as price stays range-bound
